meaning: n.最初,青春,精华adj.主要的,最初的,有青春活力的,最好的,第一流的,根本的,[数]素数的v.预先准备好,<口>让人吃(喝)足,灌注,填装
examples: The Prime Minister was in ebullient mood.
Somebody threw paint at the prime minister.
He's the police's prime suspect in this case.
Clearly, a very weak job market is the prime cause for this loss of hope.
Today it is most well known as the birthplace of wartime Prime Minister Winston Churchill.
